title: Loop Verification Result
description: Verifier output. The loop is complete only when passed is true, which requires every score value >= 8 AND a passing verifier self-test. Scores are measured objects, never bare asserted numbers: each must name the checks it was derived from. Stored at .work/semantic-loop/verification.json.

passed.description: True only if every score value is >= 8 and the verifier self-test passed.

verifier.description: Which verifier produced this result and proof that its gate can fail. A verifier that has never been observed to fail proves nothing.

tool.description: Id of the verification tool, e.g. 'verifier-v1'. Verification logic must live outside the scripts that generate the artefacts.

gallery_source.description: Gallery path the verifier was copied from, e.g. '.agent-loop/tools/verifier'.

self_test.description: Mutation self-test result: deliberately corrupted artefact copies must be caught by the gate. Null only when the run explicitly skipped self-test (never for a final PASS).

weakest_score.description: Name of the lowest-scoring dimension; null only when passed is true and no dimension is weakest-relevant.

required_next_action.description: Concrete corrective action for the next iteration; required (non-null) whenever passed is false.

gate_failures.description: One entry per dimension below 8, e.g. 'parser_validity below 8'. Empty when passed.

checks.description: The measured checks backing the scores. Every score's derived_from entries must appear here.

score.description: The score dimension this check feeds.
A measured score. 'value' must be a fixed formula over the named checks — a bare constant is a contract violation even though a schema cannot detect intent.

derived_from.description: Ids of the checks (in checks[]) this value was computed from.

measurement.description: Human-readable summary of the measured quantities behind the value.
